content:
# Dammam Dome

**Summary**: A large carbonate structure in the Eastern Province of Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] focused on the Dammam Dome to study its structural and karst features.

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used as a case study to validate the joint inversion of DCR and SR data.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] investigated the relationship between near-surface structures and the Dammam Dome.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] studied the hydrogeochemistry within the Dammam Dome structure.

## Geological and Structural Setting

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] doming mechanism and dissolution-driven karstification

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used as a case study to validate the joint inversion of DCR and SR data.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] investigated the relationship between near-surface structures and the Dammam Dome.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] studied the hydrogeochemistry within the Dammam Dome structure.

## Formations Present

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] dammam-formation, rus-formation

## Key Findings From This Location

- The dome is characterized by fractured carbonates, joints, and a history of dissolution. ([[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018, p. 17]])

- The case study in the Dammam Dome demonstrated the effectiveness of the combined optimization approach in accurately characterizing the subsurface geology. ([[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023, p. 17]])

- The dome covers an area of about 500 km2. ([[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023, p. 16]])

- The Khobar aquifer is absent at the top of the Dammam Dome where the member is eroded. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 43]])
